keyTerms.trappers: Children as young as four employed in coal mines to open and close ventilation doors, sitting alone in darkness for twelve-hour shifts
keyTerms.phossy jaw: A fatal necrosis of the jawbone caused by white phosphorus exposure, common among girls working in Victorian match factories
keyTerms.Factory Act of 1833: The first meaningful child labor legislation, prohibiting employment under age nine and limiting children to nine-hour workdays
keyTerms.parliamentary commissions: Official investigative bodies whose published reports, with testimony from child workers, galvanized middle-class support for labor reform
keyTerms.William Blake: Romantic poet whose 'Chimney Sweeper' poems exposed the suffering of child laborers and contributed to the moral case for reform
